one_time_description: Deferred revenue from ISC ($5,439,245 received in FY2023) recognized as revenue in FY2024 following significant progress on Cultural Community Centre architectural design
economic_notes: Town centre development underway within community. Spirit Bay Developments Limited Partnership (51% owned) placed in receivership subsequent to year-end for liquidation of certain real property assets. M'inuw'ilum Marina Limited Partnership (99% owned) operates band marina. Salish Strait Seafoods Ltd. (20% owned GBE).
notes: Qualified audit opinion on four grounds: (1) tangible capital assets not fully recorded per PSAS for roads, bridges, water/utility systems; (2) investments in business enterprises and limited partnerships could not be valued as financial statements unavailable; (3) town centre development expenditures subject to contingency of repayability; (4) $5,439,245 ISC deferred revenue recognized as revenue in current year — auditor uncertain if sufficient stipulations met. Spirit Bay Developments LP placed in receivership post year-end; $4,734,006 advanced with $3,200,000 valuation allowance leaving $1,534,006 net. Annual surplus of $13,105,657 is unusually large partly due to $5,439,245 deferred revenue recognition and $3,986,399 capital acquisition adjustment. expenses_infrastructure represents Community Infrastructure line of $2,988,831. expenses_enterprises represents Economic Development of $3,570,296. Ottawa Trust Fund expense of $1,633 included in totals. total_government_transfers includes ISC $3,264,670 + FNHA $2,926,911 + DFO $877,470 + Government of Canada $2,640,705 + BC Gaming $413,192 + Province of BC $248,300 = $10,371,248 less deferred revenue adjustment not applicable to transfers; osr calculated as total revenue $18,399,184 minus government transfers $9,967,956 = $8,431,228.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
